title: Baja-Style Fish Tacos
description: A Mexican-inspired dish featuring crispy battered fish, topped with fresh slaw, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime juice. Serve with warm flour tortillas.
instructions: 1. In a shallow dish, mix together flour, cornstarch, and a pinch of salt.
2. In a separate dish, whisk together lime juice, egg, and a pinch of salt.
3. Dip each piece of fish into the lime juice mixture, coating both sides, then roll in the flour mixture to coat.
4. Heat about 1/2 inch of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the fish for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
5. Meanwhile, mix together shredded cabbage, grated carrots, and chopped cilantro in a small bowl.
6. Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
7. Assemble the tacos by placing a piece of fried fish onto a warmed tortilla, followed by a spoonful of the cabbage slaw and a dollop of salsa.
